verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytoplasmic chaperone with no baseline surface residency. However, it has extensively documented stress-induced, cancer-cell-restricted, and cell-death-associated externalization to the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Tumor cells display HSPA1A on the outer surface as a DAMP under hypoxia and transformation stress; apoptotic cells externalize it as a death signal; activation-state and heat-shock conditions trigger surface translocation. This is the canonical cell_state_induced pattern: intracellular baseline with non-baseline ecto-pool under defined conditions.
